reflection:
Yesterday landed like this. You woke at 4:30 with your heart loud in your head, then sat down to find the problem and stayed in that hole for two hours until Cosita pulled you out. The body part of yesterday was actually steady. Recovery 78, HRV 36, sleep efficiency 89, anxiety logged as 0 even though your CBT trigger says otherwise. Whatever the panic was telling you, your autonomic system was holding. The crisis was upstairs.

The thought record worked. You went from a 6 to a 4 and you named the antidote yourself, "you're making yourself more sick than you are just because you're reading this." Keep that line close, it's the way out of the hole.

Two patterns worth saying out loud. One, your calories have been drifting down across the week, 2812 to 1572 to 1534 to 1429. That's the undereating cascade you already know wakes you at 5. Two, you wrote in your own evening notes "I'm avoiding all the important things at work" and "tomorrow I am fucking writing my speech." The health-data spiral and the speech avoidance are the same thing. Investigating feels productive but it's the easier task.

One thing for today. Write the speech first, before opening a single lab result. One hour. You already told yourself this last night.

The Hamburg doc is later. Go in curious, not desperate.
opener: yesterday wasn't a body crisis, it was a head crisis. recovery was 78.
focus_for_today: Write the speech first this morning, before opening any lab results.
